welcome.body: Create a workspace first, then keep chats, tools, skills, and connections organized inside it.

workspace_context.title: Workspaces keep context separate
workspace_context.body: Use one workspace for each client, project, or area of work so history and settings stay focused.

workspace.title: Name your workspace
workspace.body: This becomes the home for your first chats and AI setup.

ready.title: Ready to start
ready.body: Connect an AI provider now, or skip and configure service connections, tools, skills, and credentials from More later.

skills_tool.description_hint: Tell the AI when and how to use this tool.
skills_tool.query_hint: Add URL query values one by one. Use Liquid like {{ input.search }} or {{ credential.api_key }}.
skills_tool.body_hint: Optional Liquid template. Use {{ input.name | json }} in JSON bodies and {% if input.name %} for optional fields.
skills_tool.inputs_hint: Define the fields the AI agent must collect before running this tool.
skills_tool.input_description_hint: Explain what value the AI should provide.
skills_tool.requires_credential_hint: Require the agent to pass a credentialId before this tool can run.

skill_credentials_definitions.title: Credentials
skill_credentials_definitions.delete_confirm: Delete this credential? Skills using it will no longer require these credentials.
skill_credentials_definitions.hint: Define the credential fields that skills can use, such as api_key or user_id.

skill_credentials.missing_hint: This skill needs a credential before it can be loaded.
skill_credentials.optional_missing_hint: No credential is configured. This skill can still be loaded, but tools that require credentials will stay unavailable.

skills_manager.title: Skills Manager
skills_manager.description: Create and edit user skills and skill template tools.
skills_manager.content:
Use this skill to create, inspect, edit, and delete user skills, skill template tools, and skill credential definitions.
When creating a skill, add the skill instructions, any needed template tool definitions, and any needed credential definitions.
Set isCredentialOptional true only when the skill can be loaded without a saved credential.
Set requiresCredential true on each tool that cannot run without a credentialId.
Agents must choose credentials explicitly with list_skill_credentials and pass credentialId; the app never auto-picks credentials.
Inspect existing workspace skills, template tools, and credential definitions before creating new records.
Use Liquid templates for URL, query, headers, and body: {{ input.name }}, {{ credential.apiKey }}, {% if input.location %}, and {% for item in input.items %}.
For JSON request bodies, set bodyFormat to json and use {{ input.name | json }} or {{ credential.name | json }} for dynamic JSON values.
Use Liquid conditionals for optional or dependent fields. Do not use {input:name}, {credential:name}, or {{name}} in new templates.
Define each tool input accurately with type, description, and optional when appropriate.
Only create user skills from explicit user intent.
